Title: Enterprise | DBA | DB Administration (S149)
Description:
Summary Description: 

As a Database Administrator, I want to handle administrative duties for database environments promoting performance, scalability, compliance, and effective governance while acting as the backbone that supports the entire data infrastructure.

Title: DBA 1| DCL | REVIEW | Audit Role Security - bibaoprodazadb1.bibapollodb (Week 1)
Description:
Summary Description of Change:

Title: DBA 1| DDL | REVIEW | Validate Database Management LSOs (Week 1)
Description:
Summary Description:

As a Database Administrator (DBA), I want to ensure that the databases I am responsible for supporting adhere to the "CIA" principles of Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability.

For this user story, I want to be able to comprehensively manage biBerk's database environment, which consists of multiple database platforms, through a unified orchestration layer, to ease the burden of administration required to keep the environments performing optimally. 

This orchestration layer is comprised of >250 LSOs as of the creation of this task.

These LSOs should be regularly validated to ensure that connectivity remains uninterrupted.

Title: DBA 3| DBCC | CHECKDB | Integrity Consistency - bibaoprodazadb1.bibapollodb (Week 1)
Description:
Summary Description of Change:

As a Database Administrator (DBA), I want to ensure that the databases I am responsible for supporting adhere to the "CIA" principles of Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability.  

This task entails executing the DBCC CHECKDB console command against a copy of critical databases to verify the logical and physical integrity of all the objects within the database, validates the contents of every indexed view in the database, and validates the link-level consistency between table metadata and system locations when storing varbinary(max) data.

Title: DBA 2| IaC | REVIEW | bicep Configuration Files (Week 1)
Description:
Summary Description:

As a Database Administrator (DBA), I want to ensure that the databases I am responsible for supporting adhere to the "CIA" principles of Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability.

Many attributes within the biBerk Database Environment follow an "Infrastructure as Code" paradigm, some of which are controlled via Azure bicep configuration files and are subject to silent modifications, or modifications that are partially obscured from the Database Services function.  Keeping that in mind, regularly review the bicep configuration files that inform these modifications, ensuring that no unexpected or incorrect configurations are present.

DO NOT CHANGE ATTRIBUTES WITHOUT EXPLICITLY COLLABORATING WITH MEMBERS OF THE DevOps FUNCTION.

Title: DBA 3| DDL | UPDATE | Rebuild Statistics - bibaoprodazadb1.bibapollodb (Week 1)
Description:
Summary Description of Change:

As a Database Administrator (DBA), I want to ensure that the databases I am responsible for supporting adhere to the "CIA" principles of Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability.  
In order to ensure that queries, routines, and processes executed against objects within a critical database are as performant as possible, the statistics that are gathered during database operations should be rebuilt in light of the constantly changing state of a database.

NOTE: Table Variables do not generate statistics.  Therefore, when query plans are generated and referred to during database operations, any historical use of table variables is not considered.  Datasets that vary in size, and are stored in Table Variables should be expected to have wildly varying performance characteristics over time.

In order to ensure that the principle of least-privilege is followed within the database environment, DML statements are submitted to the DBA team within the Fresh ticketing system, and executed on behalf of the submitter after review by a DBA.

Add discussion comment / link to PBIs that track this work separately.

Title: DBA 2| DBCC | TRACEON | Review Diagnostic Flags (Week 1)
Description:
Summary Description of Change:

As a Database Administrator (DBA), I want to ensure that the databases I am responsible for supporting adhere to the "CIA" principles of Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability.  

Trace flags are used to set specific server characteristics, alter particular behaviors, and diagnose performances issues.

NOTE: This applies only to SQL Server Instances, and Azure SQL Managed Instances.  In Azure SQL Database, DBCC TRACEON / Trace Flags are not supported.
